reasoning: The math required to trigger a YES resolution strongly dictates a NO. With a 360-day baseline reference value of 1.75, Togo experienced roughly 9 'Riot' events over the entire preceding year ((1.75 - 1) * 360/30 = 9). The resolution requires more than 10x the reference value (>17.5), meaning Togo would need at least 18 ACLED-recorded riots in a single 30-day window. It is highly improbable for Togo to experience twice as many riots in a single month as it did over the entire preceding year, especially considering the past year included a major, deadly constitutional crisis and highly contested elections in mid-2025. Given that the situation has since cooled down and October 2025 shows no signs of massive, daily riot events, the first two resolution windows (Nov 2025) will almost certainly not meet the target. The final resolution date (Jan 2026) has slightly higher uncertainty, but the structural difficulty of reaching 18+ riots keeps the probability extremely low.
